name: PPLX Chart System
description: Perplexity's chart system for social posts and research-blog figures. Two surfaces, one rulebook.

principles.rhythm_contract: Every card: head pins top, content pins above footer, footer pins bottom, breath lives in the middle 1fr slot (gap:4cqi enforced).
principles.one_accent_per_card: Teal 300 is the punchline. Magenta 400 only for failure states. Indigo 400 only when a research multi-line plot needs a second series. Warm grey for the third series.
principles.hero_in_ink: Hero numbers always render in ink (--text-dark-1 on dark, --ink on light), never in any palette color.
principles.type_scale_via_cqi: Every type-size declaration uses cqi (container query inline units), never px. Cards must scale with their container.
principles.italic_opsz_cap: GT Canon Italic opsz is capped at 60 (the variable font's actual design space). Pushing past it produces sloppy rendering.
principles.pplx_mark_position: PPLX 2026 mark is 36×36 absolute top-right on every card. Color follows --text-dark-1 so it inverts cleanly across surfaces.
principles.g_archetype_face: G archetypes use GT Canon as title face, not Mono. Mono is utility chrome only.

teal_300.use: The leading accent. Highlighted bar, hero ranked entry, peak point, callout chip. Used once per card.

magenta_400.use: Failure states and warning strips. fit.js data-fit-warn uses it. Reserved — do not use decoratively.

indigo_400.use: Second series in research-variant multi-line plots only. Pairs with Teal 300 across a value axis.

warm_grey.use: Editorial-light surface baseline; third series in research multi-line plots. Reads quiet against ink.

spacing_scale.min_breath: 4cqi gap on body grid (enforced even if 1fr collapses)

A.name: Stat + bar motif
A.use_when: One hero number plus a comparison ranking of 7-9 entries.
A.notes: One bar gets class='bm-bar hi' — the focal entry. Other bars stay teal-dim.

B.name: 2×2 metric grid
B.use_when: Exactly 4 independent metrics. No hero stack — cells share the load.
B.notes: Mark the leading cell with class='b-cell hi'.

C.name: Comparison pair
C.use_when: Before/after, A vs B, two states with a delta.
C.notes: Right side gets teal accent. Hero summarizes the delta (e.g. '7.5×' or '+18pp').

D.name: Dot-matrix
D.use_when: Multi-category rhythm where shape matters more than precision. 7+ columns.
D.notes: One column marked class='peak' contains a class='d-dot hi' (teal). Other dots are .d-dot or .d-dot.mid.

E.name: Editorial stat block
E.use_when: One hero number plus 3 supporting metrics. Hero dominates upper half, triad pins low.
E.notes: 4:5 gives more vertical authority than 1:1. Use for the strongest social cards.

F.name: Wallet list
F.use_when: Ranked list of 4-7 entries with one entry promoted to lead. Stories format.
F.notes: Lead row is promoted regardless of canonical rank. Long names can use <span class='soft'> on the suffix.

G-display.name: Cover claim
G-display.notes: GT Canon Narrow Medium. Use text-wrap:balance for natural line breaks. 80 char max.

G-editorial.name: Framed claim
G-editorial.use_when: Essay opener or longer headline. Reads as editorial type.
G-editorial.notes: GT Canon Regular for the title. Hairline rule glyph between title and deck. 240 char max for deck.

G-quote.name: Italic attribution
G-quote.use_when: Attributed pull quote with Sans cite line.
G-quote.notes: GT Canon Italic, opsz 60 (cap). Use smart quotes “ ”, not straight. 200 char max for quote. Rule glyph replaces em-dash before attribution.

decision_tree.tiebreaker: If two archetypes fit, pick the one whose aspect ratio matches the distribution channel. Stories → 9:16. Feed posts → 1:1 or 4:5. Twitter/X cards → 16:9.

skill_usage.fetch_pattern: 1. Fetch manifest.json once. 2. Use decision_tree to pick archetype. 3. Fetch that archetype's template_url. 4. Substitute placeholders. 5. Optionally fetch fit_js for validation.
skill_usage.placeholder_syntax: Double-brace: {{ name }}. Whitespace inside braces is tolerated.
skill_usage.minimum_fetches: manifest.json + one template_url (templates reference _base.css by absolute URL).
skill_usage.validation: Load fit.js after render. fit.js attaches data-fit-warn attributes on any element that breaks the archetype contract (horizontal-overflow, density-mismatch, missing-structure). Add class='fit-debug' on <body> to render a magenta strip on warning elements.
skill_usage.stability: Templates and base CSS are versioned via the version field above. The base_url is permanent; the version pins the schema. For frozen builds, fetch via the GitHub raw URL at a tagged commit instead of the Pages URL.
